title: Pin ML dependency versions and generate uv lock file
analysis:
The pyproject.toml currently lists 25+ runtime dependencies, almost all unpinned (only moviepy>=2.0 has a lower bound). ML dependencies like whisperx, ultralytics, insightface, faster-whisper, transformers, audiocraft are especially volatile across major versions. The task requires adding lower-bound + major-version cap constraints (>=X.Y,<Z.0) for ML deps while leaving stable deps (click, pyyaml, requests) unpinned. Then generate uv.lock via `uv lock` for reproducible installs.

The existing test_scaffolding.py has test_pyproject_toml_has_all_dependencies which normalizes dep names by stripping version specifiers before checking — this test will continue to pass after adding version constraints. The CI workflow already uses `uv sync --extra dev` which will automatically use uv.lock when present.

Key considerations:
- The task description gives suggested version ranges; actual values should be based on what's installed or reasonable defaults for the project.
- No exact pinning (==) — lower bounds + major caps strike the right balance.
- scenedetect, panns-inference, qwen-vl-utils, faiss-gpu, scikit-learn, kokoro, pyav also fall in the ML/specialized category and should get constraints.
- Stable deps (click, numpy, Pillow, pyyaml, requests, anthropic, google-api-python-client) can remain unpinned or get light lower bounds only.

steps.description: Add test_ml_deps_have_version_constraints to test_scaffolding.py: parse pyproject.toml, identify the ML dependency list (whisperx, faster-whisper, ultralytics, insightface, transformers, audiocraft, moviepy, opentimelineio, scenedetect, panns-inference, kokoro, pyav, qwen-vl-utils, faiss-gpu, scikit-learn), and assert each has both a lower bound (>=) and an upper bound (<) version constraint. This test will initially fail because deps are unpinned.

Add test_stable_deps_not_overconstrained to test_scaffolding.py: verify that stable deps (click, pyyaml, requests, numpy, Pillow, anthropic, google-api-python-client) do NOT have upper-bound constraints (<). They may optionally have lower bounds. This test should pass immediately since those deps are currently unpinned.

Update pyproject.toml: add lower-bound + major-cap version constraints for all ML/volatile dependencies. Use the version ranges from the task description, adjusted for what's reasonable: whisperx>=0.10,<1.0; ultralytics>=8.0,<9.0; insightface>=0.7,<1.0; faster-whisper>=1.0,<2.0; transformers>=4.40,<5.0; audiocraft>=1.3,<2.0; moviepy>=2.0,<3.0 (already has >=2.0); opentimelineio>=0.17,<1.0; scenedetect>=0.6,<1.0; panns-inference>=0.1,<1.0; kokoro>=0.9,<1.0; pyav>=12.0,<14.0; qwen-vl-utils>=0.0.8,<1.0; faiss-gpu>=1.7,<2.0; scikit-learn>=1.4,<2.0. Keep click, numpy, Pillow, pyyaml, requests, anthropic, google-api-python-client unpinned.
Add test_uv_lock_file_exists to test_scaffolding.py: assert that uv.lock file exists at the project root. This test will initially fail because uv.lock doesn't exist yet.
Add test_uv_lock_not_gitignored to test_scaffolding.py: read .gitignore and assert it does not contain 'uv.lock'. This test should pass immediately since .gitignore doesn't mention uv.lock.

Run `uv lock` in the project root to generate the uv.lock file. Verify the generated file exists and is non-empty. If uv lock fails due to resolution conflicts, adjust version constraints in pyproject.toml and retry.

Run the full test suite (pytest tests/test_scaffolding.py) to verify all new and existing tests pass: test_ml_deps_have_version_constraints, test_stable_deps_not_overconstrained, test_uv_lock_file_exists, test_uv_lock_not_gitignored, and all pre-existing tests (especially test_pyproject_toml_has_all_dependencies which normalizes dep names).

design_decisions.decision: Use >=X.Y,<Z.0 pattern (lower bound + major cap) rather than exact pinning
design_decisions.rationale: Per task description: exact pinning (==) is too rigid for a solo project. Lower bounds ensure minimum feature set; major caps prevent breaking API changes. This is the standard pattern for ML libraries that break across major versions.

design_decisions.decision: Categorize deps into ML/volatile (constrained) vs stable (unconstrained)
design_decisions.rationale: ML libraries (whisperx, ultralytics, transformers, etc.) frequently have breaking changes across majors. Stable ecosystem libraries (click, pyyaml, requests, numpy) follow semver reliably and rarely break. Over-constraining stable deps creates unnecessary resolver conflicts.

design_decisions.decision: Add tests to test_scaffolding.py rather than a new test file
design_decisions.rationale: test_scaffolding.py already contains pyproject.toml validation tests (test_pyproject_toml_valid, test_pyproject_toml_has_all_dependencies). Version constraint tests and uv.lock existence tests are natural extensions of this scaffolding validation.

design_decisions.decision: Include scenedetect, panns-inference, kokoro, pyav, qwen-vl-utils, faiss-gpu, scikit-learn in constrained set
design_decisions.rationale: These are ML/specialized packages that can break across majors. The task description lists a subset; we expand to all ML-adjacent deps for consistency. Better to constrain proactively than discover breakage later.
Per task description: uv.lock must be git-tracked for reproducible installs. This is the standard practice — lock files belong in version control for applications (as opposed to libraries).

reuse.what: Existing pyproject.toml test helpers in test_scaffolding.py
reuse.how: test_pyproject_toml_has_all_dependencies already parses pyproject.toml and normalizes dep names. New tests follow the same pattern of reading pyproject.toml with tomllib and iterating over the dependencies list.
All new tests use project_root to locate pyproject.toml, uv.lock, and .gitignore — same fixture used by existing scaffolding tests.

reuse.what: CI workflow already uses uv sync
reuse.how: No CI changes needed. `uv sync --extra dev` will automatically use uv.lock when present, giving CI reproducible installs.
